name: Luke AFB
tagline: 'Home of the Fighter Pilot,' the Air Force's premier F-35 and F-16 training base in the Phoenix West Valley.
_note: Air Force fighter-pilot training base (Glendale/West Valley, Phoenix metro AZ; Arizona does not observe DST -> America/Phoenix). CONUS full template (BAH MHA AZ013 Phoenix; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 56th Fighter Wing trains F-35A and F-16 pilots; 'Home of the Fighter Pilot.' Medical: 56th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (luke.af.mil, 56 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Arizona is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 85340 (Litchfield Park).

data.Mission: F-35A Lightning II and F-16 fighter pilot training
data.Note: Arizona does not observe daylight saving time

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Luke Air Force Base is the Air Force's premier fighter-pilot training base, home to the 56th Fighter Wing, which trains F-35A and F-16 pilots. Known as the 'Home of the Fighter Pilot,' its skies are busy with training sorties year-round.
It sits in the West Valley of metro Phoenix near Glendale, in the warm, sunny Sonoran Desert, with all the amenities of a major metropolitan area close by.

sections.title: West Valley Living in Metro Phoenix

data.subtitle: Luke is in the growing, affordable West Valley of Phoenix. The main choice is which West Valley suburb fits your commute and schools, with the whole metro within reach.
intro: The West Valley is one of metro Phoenix's fast-growing, relatively affordable areas, so BAH stretches reasonably and there's lots of newer housing. On-base housing is available; many live off base nearby.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across the West Valley suburbs, with the desert sun (and hot summers) part of the deal.

recommendations.advice: Litchfield Park, Goodyear, and Glendale sit near the base for a short commute.
Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, and Peoria offer newer neighborhoods and well-regarded schools.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the growing local market.
Summers are very hot; budget for cooling and enjoy the excellent fall, winter, and spring.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Luke AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Luke AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Litchfield Elementary, Agua Fria Union High, Dysart, and other West Valley districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via the Phoenix-area network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the growing, relatively affordable West Valley makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the metro before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The growing West Valley market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a suburb and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the growing, relatively affordable West Valley makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on the West Valley suburbs before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Arizona vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Litchfield, Agua Fria, Dysart, or another West Valley district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 56th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is Luke AFB known for?
data.a: It's the Air Force's premier fighter-pilot training base, the 'Home of the Fighter Pilot,' where the 56th Fighter Wing trains F-35A and F-16 pilots.
On-base housing is available; many live off base in the West Valley. Litchfield Park, Goodyear, Surprise, and Peoria are popular with families for schools and newer homes.
Warm, sunny Sonoran Desert: hot summers but excellent fall, winter, and spring. Note Arizona doesn't observe daylight saving time.
Luke is MHA AZ013 (Phoenix). Use the calculator above for your grade.
